verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: Tests are green (119 passed). Production lint clean. Loop 5 partially resolved F-005: extracted the three self-contained phases of run_review() into named helpers (_setup_gemini_config, _build_stdin, _build_session_data), dropping the body from ~405 to 328 LoC. The two largest phases (the two-attempt subprocess loop, and the codex-home setup that mutates session_id/use_resume) were left inline because honest extraction fails the SPT honesty bar (5+ mutable locals in+out). The flat-but-cohesive remainder is the honest state.

architecture_quality.proof: run_review.py:202 (_setup_gemini_config), :249 (_build_stdin), :265 (_build_session_data) improve Locality without new interfaces; run_review.py:457 (PROVIDERS[reviewer]['build_cmd'] registry dispatch); run_review.py:25-69 (clean import DAG). The attempt-loop phase remains inline (Finding #1: cannot extract honestly), so the coordinating function is still larger than a 9-anchor structure.
architecture_quality.residual_blocking_10: attempt-loop + codex-setup phases remain inline in run_review()
architecture_quality.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: F-005 (carried — residual phases at honesty ceiling)

state_management.proof: run_review.py:187 (_active_proc module-level single writer, read by _signal_handler at :192); the attempt loop (sole writer of _active_proc) was deliberately left inline this loop so the single-writer invariant is preserved by construction; local state machine use_resume/session_id/fallback_used scoped to the function.

domain_modeling.proof: _build_session_data at run_review.py:265 now names the session-dict assembly as one place but it remains an untyped dict (no smart constructor); PROVIDERS registry from _common is the core model; REVIEWER_ALIASES dict at run_review.py:73 normalized at the CLI boundary.
domain_modeling.residual_blocking_10: no discriminated type for provider-specific output / session dict
domain_modeling.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: CLI adapter domain is thin by design; no discriminated provider-output type is an accepted residual (thin-by-design pattern)

data_flow.proof: run_review.py:25-69 (clean DAG: all imports from _common or stdlib, no cycles); extracted helpers take explicit inputs/outputs (_setup_gemini_config(args, env) mutates only the shared env accumulator by design; _build_session_data is pure); temporal coupling still mitigated by pre-reading output_content before extract_text_from_output overwrites the file.

framework_idioms.proof: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed! (held from loop 4). The smaller composed helper functions are more idiomatic Python than the prior flat body. Held at 6.5 because test-file violations (E401, I001, RUF100 on stdlib import lines; F405 from star imports in _helpers.py) remain pre-existing infrastructure debt.
framework_idioms.residual_blocking_10: test-file lint violations (E401/I001/RUF100/F405) remain as pre-existing infrastructure debt
framework_idioms.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Test file violations are pre-existing test-only infrastructure debt not in production code; accepted residual at 6.5

concurrency.proof: Signal SIGTERM/SIGINT save/restore in try/finally and the _active_proc set/clear ordering were untouched by the extractions — the attempt loop was left inline precisely to preserve them; subprocess.Popen with communicate() blocks correctly; gemini + agy temp cleanup in finally.

simplicity.proof: run_review() body dropped from ~405 LoC (loop 4 commit d1bdfb4) to 328 LoC this loop; three cohesive phases now read as named helpers at run_review.py:202 (_setup_gemini_config), :249 (_build_stdin), :265 (_build_session_data). A reader understands each extracted phase from its name+signature without reading the whole body. Residual: attempt-loop + codex-setup phases stay inline (honest SPT downgrade — extracting threads 4-5 mutable locals in+out, adding ceremony).
simplicity.residual_blocking_10: attempt-loop + codex-setup phases inline (irreducible without ceremony per Finding #1)
simplicity.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: F-005 (carried — residual phases at honesty ceiling)

test_strategy.proof: scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py:TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests guards the arg contract; 119 tests green. The three extracted helpers are reached transitively through existing end-to-end tests in test_execution_paths.py (Indirect Interface coverage carve-out) — no new test files needed, no tests deleted.

test_strategy.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: F-006 (carried — derive make_args() from parse_args([]); not pure-subtractive)

credibility.proof: The code earns its architecture: extractions are honest (no costume layer), the SPT downgrade on the attempt-loop/codex-setup phases is recorded rather than papered over with shallow _part1()/_part2() splits; 119 tests green. Orchestrator-registry pattern, signal safety, and session isolation remain well-tested.
credibility.residual_blocking_10: attempt-loop phase still inline (irreducible without ceremony); make_args() dict manually maintained

authority_map.verdict: Split and ambiguous — two sources of truth for arg-name set; make_args() can still drift (requires two-step edit), though drift is detected immediately by TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests

allowed_writers: run_review.run_review() (sets/clears in the attempt loop + finally block, both left inline this loop)
authority_map.verdict: Single and clear — one writer, one reader, idiomatic Python signal-handler pattern. The single-writer invariant is preserved this loop because the writing block was not extracted.

authority_map.owner: session JSON file (written by save_session() from _common.session)
allowed_writers: run_review.run_review() via save_session() (dict assembled by _build_session_data at :265, written by the caller)
authority_map.verdict: Single and clear — serialized via file; run_review() is the sole writer per execution. _build_session_data is pure assembly (no I/O), so the single-writer property holds.

strengths: run_review.py delegates all provider-specific logic to _common.providers.PROVIDERS: command construction, cap flags, effort maps, session ID extraction. Orchestrator dispatches through registry (run_review.py:457).
Gemini config-overlay isolation is behavioral and tested; after this loop the logic lives in one named helper (_setup_gemini_config), unchanged in behavior.

Resume/fallback state tracked via local variables (use_resume, fallback_used), never by mutating args — and that is exactly why the attempt loop resists honest extraction (locals read after the loop).

The SPT honesty bar was applied as a gate, not a formality: two phases that would have produced ceremony-laden helpers (5+ inout locals) were left inline with the reasoning recorded, instead of shallow _part1()/_part2() splits.
CLI arg contract guarded by TestArgContract.test_make_args_keys_match_parse_args_dests — the next arg addition surfaces immediately in the suite.

findings.title: run_review() attempt-loop + codex-capture phases remain inline (irreducible without ceremony)
findings.architectural_test_failed: Shallow module (a _run_attempt() helper with 6 inputs + a 4-tuple output would have Interface approx Implementation — no Depth, only relocation; its deletion test would pass, signalling a pass-through)
findings.why_it_matters: After this loop's three extractions run_review() is 328 lines (down from ~405). The two largest remaining phases — the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup — are cohesive concerns a reader would prefer to grasp by name, but extracting them honestly requires threading 4-5 mutable locals both into and out of the helper.
findings.what_is_wrong: The attempt loop reads and mutates use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, and returncode across iterations, and those values are read again after the loop (session-id capture reads session_id; _build_session_data reads fallback_used). The codex-setup block mutates session_id and use_resume on its fail-closed path. Extracting either means a 4-tuple return plus 6+ parameters — ceremony that fails SPT Q2 and Q3.
evidence: run_review.py attempt loop: the resume-fallback branch sets use_resume = False; session_id = None; fallback_used = True; continue — then post-loop code at the session-id capture + _build_session_data call sites reads those same locals.
Per the contest HONESTY BAR and method.md SPT fake-clean anti-examples, a split that renames region comments into shallow helpers is fake simplification and is rejected.
findings.leverage_impact: Low (callers invoke run_review() cleanly; the residual flat region is internal)
findings.locality_impact: Moderate (the attempt-loop and codex-setup phases must still be read in the function body rather than understood from a signature)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A perfect 9-anchor would have every cohesive phase behind a named helper. The honest finding is that these two phases cannot reach that bar without ceremony, so the dimension is held at 6.5/7 rather than inflated — the contest-grade ceiling absent a costume layer.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Either accept the inline phases as the honest state (recommended — they earn placement per the deletion test), OR return a small frozen result type CodexCapture(home, capture_enabled, sessions_before, clear_session) from the codex-setup phase and have the caller apply the session_id = None; use_resume = False clear, converting one inout pair to a value return (marginal win; defer unless re-prioritized).
findings.blast_radius: scripts/run_review.py only (if pursued). Avoid: all other files, _common/**.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: The three extractions (_setup_gemini_config, _build_stdin, _build_session_data) pass SPT Q1-Q5: each fixes real low-Locality (a cohesive phase was inlined), is the smallest honest fix (a single named helper with explicit inputs->outputs), adds no duplicate layer (module-internal privates), keeps runtime behavior identical (119 tests green, same count), and improves the product (a reader navigates by name).
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: The gemini-overlay phase (~40 lines) and the 25-field session-dict assembly are the two highest-payoff extractions; _build_stdin is small but removes an if-nest from the body.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not extract the attempt loop or codex-setup into helpers (fails the SPT honesty bar — 4-5 inout locals). Do not touch test files. Do not pursue F-006 as 'delete the dict' — it is not pure-subtractive.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: 119 tests pass (same count). No tests added or deleted; extracted helpers covered transitively (Indirect Interface coverage carve-out).

backlog.title: Decompose run_review() flat body — residual phases
backlog.why_it_matters: Three cohesive phases were extracted this loop (simplicity 6->6.5); the attempt-loop + codex-setup phases remain inline because honest extraction adds ceremony.
backlog.score_impact: simplicity already +0.5 (6->6.5) this loop; further movement requires accepting the inline phases (promote to accepted residual) or the marginal CodexCapture value-return

backlog.title: Derive make_args() from parse_args([]) directly
backlog.why_it_matters: Would eliminate the two-step edit requirement. Correction to prior framing: NOT pure-subtractive — parse_args() takes no argv param and reads sys.argv; make_args() defaults reviewer='claude' whereas parse_args([]) yields reviewer=None, so the swap changes the test default and fails SPT Q4 unless every call site is updated. The loop-3 sync test already detects drift.
backlog.score_impact: test_strategy +0.5 (7.5->8) if done cleanly; lower priority given the not-pure-subtractive correction

deepening_candidates.candidate_module: the inline codex CODEX_HOME setup block in run_review()
deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding #1 — the block mutates session_id/use_resume inout, which is why it resisted extraction this loop.
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: inout mutation of two locals is the only thing blocking a clean extraction; converting to a value return removes it
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: per-run home reuse-or-create + capture-var derivation + the fail-closed clear decision
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: same end-to-end tests in test_execution_paths.py (codex stale-events + last-message paths already exercise this block transitively)
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: introduce CodexCapture = namedtuple(...); have the helper return it; caller applies session_id = None; use_resume = False when clear_session is set
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: do not extract the two-attempt loop (4 inout locals — irreducible); do not add a protocol or class hierarchy

builder_notes.pattern: SPT honesty bar as an extraction gate
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A candidate helper that needs 5+ mutable locals both in and out is line-relocation, not Locality — its deletion test passes, which is the tell that it's a pass-through.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Extract only clean-boundary phases (explicit inputs->outputs); for the rest, record the inout-threading count as the rejection reason and leave the phase inline.
builder_notes.stack_example: run_review() had ~6 candidate phases; 3 had clean boundaries (gemini overlay, stdin, session-dict assembly), 3 threaded mutable locals (attempt loop, codex setup, multi-input session-id capture). Extracted the 3 clean ones only.

builder_notes.pattern: Indirect Interface coverage carve-out for private helpers
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: New module-level privates reached only through an already-tested public function need no new test files.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Private helpers transitively covered by already-green Interface tests need no new test files (carve-out); do not delete the existing tests.
builder_notes.stack_example: _setup_gemini_config / _build_stdin / _build_session_data are covered by test_run_review_gemini_effort_overlay_preserves_existing_settings, test_run_review_gemini_pipes_prompt_via_stdin, test_agy_prepends_readonly_preamble_to_prompt, and every session.json read-back; each assertion fails if the helper body were a no-op.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: Three borderline claims: (1) simplicity:6.5 UP — a stricter reviewer could hold at 6 (77 lines moved, still 328 LoC); (2) architecture:7 SAME — one could argue 7.5; (3) Finding #1 'irreducible' — the CodexCapture value-return is a real marginal path, so 'irreducible' is slightly strong for the codex block (the attempt loop genuinely is).
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Surface uncertainty in builder notes rather than inflating the score; record marginal correction paths as deepening candidates instead of asserting impossibility.

narrative: Loop 5 is the honest-partial decomposition of F-005. Three cohesive phases of run_review() — the Gemini config overlay (_setup_gemini_config), the stdin prompt prep (_build_stdin), and the 25-field session-dict assembly (_build_session_data) — are extracted into named module-level helpers, dropping the function body from ~405 to 328 lines and moving simplicity from 6 to 6.5. The decisive judgment was what NOT to extract: the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup thread 4-5 mutable locals (use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, returncode) both into and out of any candidate helper, so extracting them would add ceremony and fail the SPT — they are left inline with the rejection recorded, not papered over with shallow _part1()/_part2() splits. This preserves the risk-boundary invariants the directive named: _active_proc single writer, SIGTERM/SIGINT save/restore ordering, env isolation, and resume/fresh semantics all live in the un-extracted attempt loop and finally block, so the green 119-test suite is direct evidence they held. F-005 is carried forward (cheap wins taken; remainder at the honesty ceiling). F-006 is carried with a correction: it is not the 'pure subtractive dict deletion' prior loops assumed — parse_args([]) flips the test's reviewer default and needs an argv seam, so it fails SPT Q4 as specified.

loop_result.what_changed: Partially resolved F-005 in scripts/run_review.py by extracting the three genuinely self-contained phases of run_review() into named module-level private helpers: _setup_gemini_config(args, env) -> str|None (the ~40-line Gemini temp config overlay), _build_stdin(reviewer, prompt_file) -> str|None (prompt read + agy read-only preamble), and _build_session_data(args, session, meta, reviewer, new_session_id, fallback_used) -> dict (the 25-field resume-metadata session dict assembly). The run_review() body dropped from ~405 LoC to 328 LoC. The two largest phases (the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup) were deliberately left inline because extracting either threads 4-5 mutable locals (use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, returncode) both into and out of the helper, failing the SPT honesty bar.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed! python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/ -> 119 passed, 0 failed (unchanged count). run_review() body LoC dropped from ~405 (loop 4 commit d1bdfb4) to 328 (verified via awk span measurement). Risk-boundary preservation (Meta-Rule 4): _active_proc single writer, signal save/restore ordering, env['CODEX_HOME'] mutation, and resume/fresh semantics all remain in the un-extracted attempt loop + finally block — none crossed a helper boundary, so the invariants are preserved by construction and the green suite is the primary evidence.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks passed: Reality (ruff exits 0 post-diff; pytest 119 passed; the three helpers exist at run_review.py:202/249/265 and are called from run_review(); body LoC dropped from ~405 to 328 via awk span measurement); Honesty (no new seam — helpers are module-internal privates; the SPT downgrade on the attempt-loop/codex-setup phases is recorded with the inout-threading reason rather than forced into shallow splits; the three extractions are behavior-preserving pure relocations; _build_session_data is pure assembly; the simplicity delta cites a concrete LoC drop against d1bdfb4); Regression (extractions preserve _active_proc single-writer, signal save/restore ordering, env isolation, and resume/fresh semantics — all in the un-extracted attempt loop; 119 tests green, same count; no new findings at equal-or-higher severity).
